title: Guest-mode checklist UI + upgrade CTA + consistent guest restriction messaging

requirements.summary: Add a responsive Guest Mode access checklist component with exact visible/locked labels for dashboard display.
acceptanceCriteria: When the app is in Guest Mode (as determined by existing isGuest logic), the dashboard shows a clearly readable Guest Mode checklist with the exact labels specified.
The checklist renders as a multi-column grid on wider screens and a sidebar/stacked layout on small screens without overflow or layout breakage.
No user-facing label text in the checklist is missing, renamed, or paraphrased.

file_operations.description: Create a GuestModeChecklist component that renders two sections (Visible to guests / Locked for guests) with the exact labels provided, using a responsive layout (multi-column grid on desktop; stacked/sidebar-style on small screens). Use existing shadcn/ui primitives (e.g., Card, Badge, Separator, Button) from frontend/src/components/ui/* for consistent styling. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Wire GuestModeChecklist into the dashboard UI so it is shown when isGuest=true, positioned in a layout-ready location (without changing overall dashboard navigation). Ensure the checklist area remains readable across breakpoints.

requirements.summary: Add a guest-upgrade CTA on the checklist that opens the existing ProfileSetupModal in guest-upgrade mode without leaving the dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: From the guest-mode checklist, clicking the CTA opens ProfileSetupModal with upgrade copy (isGuestUpgrade=true).
Closing/dismissing the modal returns the user to the same dashboard state without a full page reload.

file_operations.description: Add a clear upgrade CTA ("Create an account" / "Sign in to unlock") to GuestModeChecklist and expose an onUpgrade callback so the dashboard can open ProfileSetupModal without navigation. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Add optional controlled close support (e.g., onClose callback and Dialog onOpenChange handling) so the modal can be opened/closed from the dashboard CTA and dismissed cleanly back to the dashboard state. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Add local state to open/close ProfileSetupModal from the GuestModeChecklist CTA (isGuestUpgrade=true) and ensure dismissing the modal preserves the current dashboard tab/state without reload.

Update GuestBanner’s ProfileSetupModal usage to pass the new optional onClose behavior (if introduced) so banner and checklist upgrade flows behave consistently. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Ensure guest users cannot save/persist or export data for locked features, with a consistent upgrade-required toast/message on blocked actions.
acceptanceCriteria: In Guest Mode, attempting any blocked action results in a clear error/toast message telling the user to create an account to save/unlock the feature.
DataLogging export is blocked for guests even when there are entries available, and the user is informed it requires a full account.
Diagnostic session persistence/export actions are blocked in Guest Mode, and the user is informed it requires a full account.
Non-blocked read-only areas remain accessible in Guest Mode (Study, Videos, Parts & Specs, Suppliers, and limited AI assistant usage where no saving occurs).

file_operations.description: Create a small shared helper (e.g., a single exported constant message string and guard function) for consistent guest blocked-action messaging ("Create an account to save/unlock this feature.") and reuse it across restricted UI actions.

Block CSV export when isGuest=true (even if logEntries exist) by intercepting the export action and showing the shared guest restriction message; ensure the action does not generate/download a file for guests.

Add guest guards around diagnostic persistence and any export/download/report actions so isGuest=true prevents saving diagnostic sessions and exporting diagnostic reports, and shows the shared guest restriction message instead.
Ensure any remaining job create/save UI actions are consistently blocked for isGuest=true with the shared guest restriction message (do not change read-only viewing if present).

Plumb isGuest into community-related modules where posting/saving may occur so guest users see Community as locked (or have posting/saving disabled) with the shared guest restriction message when they attempt a blocked action.
